foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Cholangitis presents as infected obstructive jaundice; understanding biliary obstruction pathophysiology is essential for recognizing Charcot triad and Reynolds pentad

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: CBD stones are the most common cause of acute cholangitis; stone impaction leads to biliary stasis and subsequent bacterial infection

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Knowledge of biliary tree anatomy (hepatic ducts, CBD, ampulla) is critical for understanding obstruction sites and planning ERCP or surgical drainage

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Gallstones migrate from gallbladder to CBD causing obstructive cholangitis; shared risk factors and management pathway

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Benign and malignant strictures are the second most common cause of cholangitis after stones

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Post-cholecystectomy bile duct injury can lead to stricture formation and recurrent cholangitis

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Often coexists with cholangitis in gallstone disease; both require assessment of the biliary tree

integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Cholangitis across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
